name: Gibson Bay Golf Course
about: Gibson Bay Golf Course is an 18-hole public golf facility designed by Michael Hurdzan and opened in 1993, recognized by Golf Digest for its quality. The course is a 7,100 yard, par 72 layout located at Richmond's Lake Reba recreational complex. It features five sets of tees to accommodate all skill levels and bent grass tees, greens, and fairways for optimal playing conditions. The long, open course is known for its challenging wind-affected play, side-hill lies, and greens protected by bunkers and mounding.
why_golfers_like_it: Multiple tee boxes for all skill levels
Bent grass surfaces for superior playing conditions
On-site golf instruction and club fitting services

food_and_drink.notes: Full-service golf shop and pro shop available on-site.
Standard golf course beverage service available.

simulator_hardware.notes: Site mentions a dedicated Golf Garage for instruction and fitting, with a Golf Simulator Rental service, but provides no specific hardware details.

faq.question: Do I need to bring my own clubs?
faq.answer: Yes, you are expected to bring your own golf clubs to play the course. The facility does have a Golf Garage which offers club fitting services.

faq.question: Can I bring my own food or drinks?
faq.answer: The facility does not explicitly mention a BYOB policy. Food and beverage services are available through the pro shop and on-site concessions.

faq.question: How long does it take to play 18 holes?
faq.answer: The website does not provide an estimated time for a full 18-hole round. Pace of play can vary based on time of day and course conditions.

faq.question: Is the facility kid-friendly?
faq.answer: Yes, Gibson Bay offers specific junior golf programs and family-oriented features like the birthday club and short course, indicating a family-friendly environment.
